source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In this picture there is what I saw on four different clocks at the same time. Only one of them had the right time. One was 20 minutes fast. Another 20 minutes slow. One had stopped some time ago.
<image1>
What was the right time?
extraction_notes: choice labels inferred from options array order as A-E

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'In this picture' and asks about four different clocks shown visually
solving requires reading clock-face times from the image; those times are not enumerated in text
multi_step: must extract multiple observed times from four clocks, then apply constraints: one correct, one 20 minutes fast, one 20 minutes slow, one stopped
requires combining visual reading with logical consistency across at least four candidates
multiple-choice options are provided, but selecting among them still depends on the above constraint reasoning
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a precise time value ('5:05')
choices are provided and answer_text matches one option exactly (B: 5:05)
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, answer_present=true, and image asset exists

decision_reasons: question text, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantics are fully supported
initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
no registry issues or risk flags are reported
the answer is precise and directly verifiable against the provided multiple-choice option
